treatments.name: Surgical Resection + Adjuvant Radiation Therapy (Photon/IMRT)

evidence_level.rationale: Decades of retrospective and prospective data supporting surgery plus adjuvant RT as cornerstone of ACC management; NCCN Category 1 recommendation for salivary gland malignancies

survival_benefit.rationale: 10-year local control 84%, cause-specific survival 71%, OS 57% with surgery + adjuvant RT; 5-year OS approximately 78% for localized disease; perineural invasion presence (HR 2.98 for OS) makes adjuvant RT critical

accessibility.rationale: Universally available at all major head and neck cancer centers; standard NCCN/ESMO-recommended approach with established surgical and RT expertise

safety_profile.rationale: Tongue base surgery carries risks of dysphagia, speech impairment, and airway compromise; adjuvant RT causes xerostomia, mucositis, fibrosis, and late osteoradionecrosis; extensive perineural invasion mandates radiation of nerve pathways to skull base, increasing toxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: Universally applicable regardless of molecular profile; standard approach for all ACC histologies

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Complete surgical excision removes the primary tumor bulk while adjuvant radiation therapy (typically 60-66 Gy via IMRT) targets microscopic residual disease along surgical margins and perineural invasion pathways. For tongue base ACC with extensive perineural invasion, radiation fields must encompass relevant cranial nerve pathways back to the skull base to address the hallmark perineural spread pattern.

key_evidence.study_name: Multi-institutional retrospective analysis of surgery + adjuvant RT for head and neck ACC

treatments.name: Adjuvant Proton Beam Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: Largest published series (56 patients) with median 6.2 years follow-up demonstrates excellent outcomes; no RCT vs. photon RT but strong institutional data from Massachusetts General Hospital and other proton centers

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year OS 89%, cause-specific survival 89%, disease-free survival 78%, and locoregional control 88% in the largest proton therapy series; superior to historical photon RT series particularly for skull base/perineural targets

accessibility.rationale: Proton therapy available at approximately 40 centers in the US; requires referral and may involve travel; insurance coverage variable but improving for head and neck cancers with perineural invasion

safety_profile.rationale: 5-year cumulative incidence of grade 3+ toxicity was 15%; superior dose conformality reduces dose to critical structures (brainstem, temporal lobes, optic chiasm) vs. photon IMRT; still carries risks of mucositis and xerostomia

biomarker_match.rationale: Universally applicable regardless of molecular profile; particularly advantageous for ACC due to the need to irradiate along nerve pathways to skull base while sparing adjacent critical structures

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Proton beam therapy exploits the Bragg peak to deliver high-dose radiation precisely to the tumor bed and perineural invasion pathways while minimizing dose to surrounding critical structures. Median doses of 72.6 GyRBE are used, often with twice-daily fractionation. The superior dose conformality is particularly valuable for ACC of the tongue base where radiation fields must extend along cranial nerves to the skull base, allowing higher target doses while reducing toxicity to brainstem, temporal lobes, and optic structures.

key_evidence.study_name: Long-Term Outcomes Following Definitive or Adjuvant Proton Radiotherapy for Adenoid Cystic Carcinoma

treatments.availability: Available at specialized proton therapy centers

treatments.name: Axitinib (VEGFR TKI)

evidence_level.rationale: Randomized Phase II trial (axitinib vs observation) provides the highest level of evidence for any systemic agent in R/M ACC; statistically significant PFS benefit demonstrated

survival_benefit.rationale: 6-month PFS rate 73% vs 23% with observation; median PFS 10.8 vs 2.8 months (P<0.001); however 0% objective response rate and 100% disease control reflects disease stabilization only without tumor shrinkage

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for renal cell carcinoma; available off-label for R/M ACC; oral medication; widely available globally

safety_profile.rationale: Common adverse effects include hypertension, diarrhea, fatigue, and hand-foot syndrome; generally manageable but dose reductions frequently needed; better tolerated than some alternatives

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific biomarker required; targets VEGFR1-3; ACC commonly expresses VEGF; broadly applicable to R/M ACC regardless of MYB-NFIB or NOTCH status

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Axitinib is a potent and selective oral inhibitor of VEGFR1, VEGFR2, and VEGFR3. It suppresses tumor angiogenesis and provides disease stabilization in ACC. The randomized Phase II trial confirmed it significantly delays disease progression compared to observation, though it does not induce objective tumor responses, consistent with ACC's characteristically slow-growing biology.

key_evidence.study_name: Randomized Phase II Study of Axitinib versus Observation in Recurred or Metastatic ACC

treatments.name: Fast Neutron Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: Randomized RTOG/MRC trial demonstrated statistically significant locoregional control advantage (56% vs 25% at 10 years) over photon RT; large retrospective series of 545 patients confirms durable local control

survival_benefit.rationale: Complete tumor clearance 85% vs 33% for photons; 6-year and 10-year locoregional control rates of 84% and 79% in the 545-patient series; no OS advantage demonstrated in randomized trial (15% vs 25% at 10 years)

accessibility.rationale: Only a handful of neutron therapy facilities exist worldwide; most US centers have closed; limited to select research institutions; requires significant travel for most patients

safety_profile.rationale: Higher RBE of neutrons leads to greater normal tissue toxicity compared to photon or proton therapy; significant late effects including fibrosis, nerve damage, and soft tissue necrosis reported

biomarker_match.rationale: Universally applicable; particularly suited for unresectable or recurrent ACC where the high-LET radiation overcomes the radioresistance of ACC's characteristically slow growth rate

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Fast neutrons have a higher linear energy transfer (LET) than photons, producing dense ionization along their track that causes more complex, irreparable DNA damage. This overcomes the intrinsic radioresistance of slowly proliferating ACC cells. The higher relative biological effectiveness (RBE) of neutrons is less dependent on cell cycle phase and oxygen status, making them effective against the characteristically slow-growing ACC.

key_evidence.study_name: RTOG/MRC Randomized Trial: Neutrons vs Photons for Salivary Gland Tumors

notable_side_effects: Higher late toxicity than photon RT

treatments.name: Lenvatinib (Anti-VEGFR TKI)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II trial (n=32) met primary endpoint; corroborated by real-world Italian multicenter study (n=51); systematic review and meta-analysis of VEGFR inhibitors confirms lenvatinib as best-in-class for R/M ACC

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 15.6-21.7% (partial responses); median PFS 17.5 months in Phase II; median OS 16.1 months in real-world study; disease stabilization is primary benefit (75% SD rate) rather than tumor shrinkage; no cure potential

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for thyroid cancer; available off-label for R/M ACC with oncologist prescription; oral medication; widely available globally though off-label use may face insurance barriers

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity profile: hypertension (68%), diarrhea, fatigue, decreased appetite, weight loss, proteinuria, palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia; dose reductions required in majority of patients; limits long-term use

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific biomarker required; targets VEGFR1-3, FGFR1-4, PDGFR-alpha, RET, KIT; ACC commonly overexpresses VEGF; efficacy not linked to MYB-NFIB or NOTCH status

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Lenvatinib is an oral multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR1-3, FGFR1-4, PDGFR-alpha, RET, and KIT. In ACC, it primarily provides disease stabilization through anti-angiogenic activity. ACC tumors commonly express VEGF, and lenvatinib suppresses tumor blood supply and may directly inhibit tumor cell signaling through FGFR pathways, which are also implicated in ACC biology.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II Study of Lenvatinib in Progressive, Recurrent or Metastatic ACC

treatments.name: REM-422 (MYB mRNA Degrader)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1 ARIA trial with preliminary data showing 43% ORR in efficacy population; FDA Fast Track designation granted March 2026; first-in-class mechanism with proof-of-concept demonstrated

survival_benefit.rationale: 43% ORR is unprecedented for R/M ACC (historically 0-16% with other agents); 71% of biomarker-positive patients on study >6 months showed >20% tumor shrinkage; directly targets the MYB-NFIB fusion driver in this patient's tumor

accessibility.rationale: Currently available only through Phase 1/2 ARIA clinical trial (NCT06118086) at select centers including MSKCC and UCSF; FDA Fast Track and Orphan Drug designations may accelerate approval timeline

safety_profile.rationale: Generally well tolerated in Phase 1; most common treatment-related adverse events were anemia, fatigue, and epistaxis at RP2D; oral small molecule with favorable tolerability profile

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets MYB via mRNA degradation through poison exon incorporation; this patient is MYB-NFIB fusion positive, making them an ideal candidate; 50-70% of ACC cases harbor this fusion

treatments.mechanism_of_action: REM-422 is a first-in-class oral small molecule that degrades MYB mRNA by facilitating incorporation of a poison exon into the MYB transcript, triggering nonsense-mediated decay. This suppresses MYB protein expression, directly targeting the pathognomonic MYB-NFIB fusion oncoprotein that drives ACC proliferation and survival. The mechanism is highly specific to MYB-dependent tumors.

key_evidence.study_name: ARIA: Phase 1/2 Study of REM-422 in Adenoid Cystic Carcinoma

biomarker_requirements: MYB transcript expression with poison exon

treatments.name: Vorinostat (HDAC Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II trial completed in locally advanced, recurrent, or metastatic ACC; 97% clinical benefit rate and 75% 6-month stable disease rate; exceptional response duration (53 months) in one responder

survival_benefit.rationale: Only 2 partial responses (low ORR); however 97% clinical benefit rate and 75% 6-month SD rate reflect disease stabilization; one response lasting 53 months is notable for ACC; primarily a disease stabilization agent

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for cutaneous T-cell lymphoma; available off-label for ACC; oral medication; preclinical data shows synergy with cisplatin which may expand future use

safety_profile.rationale: Known HDAC inhibitor class effects: thrombocytopenia, fatigue, diarrhea, nausea, dysgeusia; generally manageable; tolerable for long-term use in slowly progressive ACC

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific biomarker required; HDAC inhibition may be particularly relevant in ACC given epigenetic dysregulation driven by MYB-NFIB fusion; preclinical data supports sensitization of ACC cancer stem cells to cisplatin

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Vorinostat inhibits histone deacetylases (HDACs), leading to chromatin remodeling, re-expression of silenced tumor suppressors, and induction of cell cycle arrest and apoptosis. In ACC, HDAC inhibition may counteract the epigenetic dysregulation caused by MYB-NFIB fusion-driven transcriptional reprogramming. Preclinical studies show vorinostat sensitizes ACC cancer stem cells to cisplatin and induces tumor cell senescence.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Study of Vorinostat in Locally Advanced, Recurrent, or Metastatic ACC

treatments.name: AL101 (Gamma-Secretase Inhibitor / Pan-Notch Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II ACCURACY trial completed with 82 patients enrolled; 12% ORR (9 PR) and 69% disease control rate in NOTCH-mutant R/M ACC; presented at ASCO and ESMO conferences

survival_benefit.rationale: 12% ORR with 57% stable disease in NOTCH-mutant patients; disease control rate 69%; however responses were not durable in many patients; benefit limited to the ~20% of ACC patients with NOTCH activating mutations

accessibility.rationale: ACCURACY trial completed; not yet FDA approved; follow-on trials may be available; development status uncertain following Ayala Pharmaceuticals acquisition; requires NOTCH mutation confirmation

safety_profile.rationale: Significant GI toxicity: diarrhea 78% (grade 3: 10%), fatigue 67% (grade 3: 6%), nausea 60% (grade 3: 7%); requires careful management of GI side effects

biomarker_match.rationale: This patient has NOTCH1 pathway activation, making them a potential candidate; however, AL101 was specifically tested in NOTCH1-4 mutation-positive patients; NOTCH pathway activation vs. activating mutation distinction is important for eligibility

treatments.mechanism_of_action: AL101 is a selective gamma-secretase inhibitor that blocks the proteolytic cleavage of Notch receptors, preventing release of the Notch intracellular domain (NICD) and inhibiting Notch-driven transcription. In ACC with NOTCH activating mutations (~20% of cases), constitutive Notch signaling drives tumor proliferation. AL101 disrupts this oncogenic signaling cascade. The patient's NOTCH1-activated status makes this a molecularly rational therapeutic target.

key_evidence.study_name: ACCURACY: Phase 2 Trial of AL101 in R/M ACC with NOTCH Activating Mutations
key_evidence.journal: Journal of Clinical Oncology / Annals of Oncology

biomarker_requirements: NOTCH1-4 activating mutation (for ACCURACY trial eligibility)
treatments.availability: Clinical Trial (ACCURACY trial completed; follow-on access uncertain)

treatments.name: Cyclophosphamide-Doxorubicin-Cisplatin (CAP Chemotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple retrospective series and Phase II data over decades; most-studied combination chemotherapy for ACC; ORR up to 33% but responses not durable; role is primarily palliative

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 14-33% with non-durable responses; median OS 23.4 months in recent series; chemotherapy has never demonstrated survival benefit in ACC; disease stabilization at best; ACC is fundamentally chemoresistant

accessibility.rationale: All three agents widely available globally; standard oncology regimen with established dosing protocols; no biomarker testing required; insurance coverage universal

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity: myelosuppression, cardiotoxicity (doxorubicin), nephrotoxicity (cisplatin), nausea/vomiting, alopecia, neuropathy; particularly burdensome in ACC where modest benefit does not justify significant toxicity for many patients

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker required; universally applicable; low pre-chemotherapy tumor growth rate associated with better response to CAP

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Triple cytotoxic chemotherapy combining alkylating agent (cyclophosphamide), topoisomerase II inhibitor/intercalator (doxorubicin), and platinum crosslinker (cisplatin). Each agent damages DNA through distinct mechanisms to impair tumor cell replication. However, ACC's characteristically slow proliferation rate limits chemotherapy effectiveness, as cytotoxic agents preferentially target rapidly dividing cells.

key_evidence.study_name: CAP Chemotherapy for Advanced Carcinomas of Salivary Gland Origin

treatments.name: Nivolumab + Ipilimumab (Dual Checkpoint Inhibitor Immunotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II trial (NCT03146650, n=19 ACC patients) published 2024; shows improved PFS over historical single-agent checkpoint data; represents the most promising immunotherapy approach tested in ACC

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 30.0 months and median PFS 8.3 months with 53% disease control rate; PFS superior to pembrolizumab monotherapy (4.9-6.6 months); however ACC has characteristically low PD-L1 expression and poor immunogenicity limiting checkpoint inhibitor efficacy

accessibility.rationale: Both agents FDA-approved for other indications; off-label use possible but limited insurance coverage for ACC; clinical trial enrollment preferred; NCT03146650 may still have expansion cohorts

safety_profile.rationale: Dual checkpoint inhibitor combination carries significant immune-related adverse event risk: colitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies; ipilimumab adds substantial toxicity vs. monotherapy; grade 3-4 irAE rates approximately 30-40%

biomarker_match.rationale: ACC typically has low PD-L1 expression and low tumor mutational burden, limiting checkpoint inhibitor efficacy; no established biomarker to predict response in ACC; some benefit seen regardless of PD-L1 status

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Nivolumab (anti-PD-1) and ipilimumab (anti-CTLA-4) provide complementary immune checkpoint blockade. Nivolumab reactivates exhausted T cells at the tumor interface while ipilimumab enhances T cell priming and expands the anti-tumor T cell repertoire. The dual combination aims to overcome ACC's characteristically immunosuppressive microenvironment, which features low PD-L1 expression and poor antigen presentation.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II Study of Nivolumab and Ipilimumab for Metastatic/Recurrent ACC

treatments.name: Dovitinib (FGFR/VEGFR Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II data available; 65% disease stabilization rate and median PFS 8.5 months; targets FGFR pathway which is implicated in ACC biology

survival_benefit.rationale: 65% disease stabilization; median PFS 8.5 months; no objective responses in most patients; primarily a disease-stabilizing agent; inferior PFS to lenvatinib (17.5 months)

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for renal cell carcinoma (2021); available off-label though less commonly used than lenvatinib or axitinib for ACC; oral formulation

safety_profile.rationale: Common TKI class toxicities: fatigue, nausea, diarrhea, hypertension, liver function abnormalities; moderate tolerability

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets FGFR1-3 and VEGFR1-3; ACC shows FGFR pathway alterations; no specific biomarker selection required; broadly applicable

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Dovitinib is an oral multi-kinase inhibitor targeting FGFR1-3, VEGFR1-3, PDGFR-beta, and FLT3. In ACC, it inhibits FGFR-mediated signaling implicated in tumor growth and VEGFR-mediated angiogenesis, providing dual pathway suppression.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II Study of Dovitinib in ACC

treatments.name: CB-103 + Lenvatinib (NOTCH Transcription Inhibitor Combination)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase I/II trial (NCT05774899) currently enrolling; CB-103 is an oral NOTCH transcription complex inhibitor with a distinct mechanism from gamma-secretase inhibitors; limited published efficacy data

survival_benefit.rationale: Preclinical rationale is strong for dual NOTCH + VEGFR inhibition; no mature clinical efficacy data yet available; targets two key ACC pathways simultaneously

accessibility.rationale: Available only through NCT05774899 clinical trial; Phase I/II with 34 patients planned; limited enrollment sites

safety_profile.rationale: CB-103 may avoid the severe GI toxicity of gamma-secretase inhibitors by acting downstream on NOTCH transcription rather than receptor cleavage; however combination with lenvatinib adds VEGFR TKI toxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient has both NOTCH1 activation and MYB-NFIB fusion; CB-103 targets NOTCH pathway downstream of gamma-secretase; combination with lenvatinib addresses VEGFR; dual pathway targeting is molecularly rational

treatments.mechanism_of_action: CB-103 is an oral small molecule that inhibits the NOTCH transcription complex, blocking NOTCH signaling downstream of gamma-secretase cleavage. Unlike gamma-secretase inhibitors, CB-103 may avoid goblet cell metaplasia and severe GI toxicity. Combined with lenvatinib (multi-kinase VEGFR inhibitor), this approach simultaneously targets NOTCH-driven proliferation and VEGF-mediated angiogenesis, two key oncogenic pathways in ACC.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase I/II Study of CB-103 Combined with Lenvatinib or Abemaciclib

notable_side_effects: Combination toxicity with lenvatinib (hypertension, diarrhea, fatigue)
CB-103-specific toxicity profile still being characterized

treatments.name: 177Lu-PSMA Radioligand Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II trials (NCT06322576, NCT06199453) currently recruiting; PSMA expression confirmed in 94% of primary ACC cases; strong biologic rationale but no published efficacy data in ACC yet

survival_benefit.rationale: No published efficacy data in ACC; extrapolated from prostate cancer experience where 177Lu-PSMA-617 showed significant OS benefit; high PSMA expression (94%) in ACC provides biologic rationale for targeting

accessibility.rationale: Available only through clinical trials (NCT06322576, NCT06199453); 177Lu-PSMA-617 (Pluvicto) FDA-approved for prostate cancer but off-label use in ACC not established; requires nuclear medicine infrastructure and PSMA-PET imaging

safety_profile.rationale: Known safety profile from prostate cancer: xerostomia (common and relevant for ACC patients already at risk), myelosuppression, fatigue, nausea; generally manageable; targeted delivery limits systemic toxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: PSMA expression in 94% of ACC cases provides excellent target coverage; requires PSMA-PET confirmation; independent of MYB-NFIB or NOTCH status; novel mechanism addressing an unmet therapeutic need

treatments.mechanism_of_action: 177Lu-PSMA delivers targeted beta-radiation to PSMA-expressing tumor cells. Lutetium-177 conjugated to a PSMA-binding ligand selectively irradiates tumor cells expressing prostate-specific membrane antigen on their surface. With 94% of ACC tumors expressing PSMA, this represents a novel theranostic approach leveraging the success of PSMA-targeted therapy in prostate cancer to a new indication where systemic therapy options are severely limited.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II Study of 177Lu-PSMA in ACC (recruiting)

notable_side_effects: Xerostomia (particularly relevant for ACC patients post-RT)

treatments.name: GSK3326595 (PRMT5 Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: Early phase data showing 21% partial response rate (3/14 patients) in ACC cohort; represents a novel epigenetic target; limited sample size

survival_benefit.rationale: 21% ORR (3/14 patients achieving PR) is notable for ACC where responses to systemic therapy are rare; however very small sample size limits conclusions; mechanism is scientifically compelling

accessibility.rationale: Investigational compound; available only through clinical trials; very early stage of development for ACC indication

safety_profile.rationale: PRMT5 inhibitor class: hematologic toxicity (thrombocytopenia, anemia, neutropenia), fatigue, nausea; safety profile still being characterized in ACC population

biomarker_match.rationale: PRMT5 mediates arginine methylation affecting splicing and gene expression; may interact with MYB-NFIB fusion-driven transcriptional programs; no established predictive biomarker yet

treatments.mechanism_of_action: GSK3326595 inhibits protein arginine methyltransferase 5 (PRMT5), an enzyme that catalyzes symmetric dimethylation of arginine residues on histones and other proteins. PRMT5 inhibition disrupts RNA splicing, gene expression, and DNA damage repair pathways critical for tumor cell survival. In ACC, PRMT5 inhibition may interfere with MYB-driven transcriptional programs and sensitize tumors to DNA damage.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase I/II Study of GSK3326595 in Solid Tumors Including ACC

treatments.name: OBT076 (Anti-CD205 Antibody-Drug Conjugate) +/- Balstilimab

evidence_level.rationale: Phase I trial (NCT05930951, n=32 planned) currently recruiting; antibody-drug conjugate targeting CD205 which is expressed on ACC cells; no published efficacy data yet

survival_benefit.rationale: No mature efficacy data; ADC mechanism has shown transformative results in other solid tumors (breast, urothelial); preclinical rationale for CD205 targeting in ACC is strong; optional combination with anti-PD-1 balstilimab may enhance activity

accessibility.rationale: Phase I trial only; very limited enrollment; investigational agent not available outside of clinical trial; early stage of clinical development

safety_profile.rationale: ADC class effects expected: infusion reactions, myelosuppression, neuropathy, GI toxicity; specific safety profile for OBT076 still being characterized; combination with checkpoint inhibitor adds immune-related AE risk

biomarker_match.rationale: CD205 is expressed on ACC tumors; no requirement for MYB-NFIB or NOTCH status; may complement molecular profile of this patient; novel targeting mechanism independent of existing pathways

treatments.mechanism_of_action: OBT076 is an antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) targeting CD205, a C-type lectin receptor expressed on ACC tumor cells. The ADC delivers a cytotoxic payload directly to CD205-expressing cells, combining targeted antibody specificity with potent chemotherapy. Optional combination with balstilimab (anti-PD-1 checkpoint inhibitor) may enhance anti-tumor immunity through immunogenic cell death induced by the ADC payload.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase I Study of OBT076 +/- Balstilimab in Solid Tumors Including ACC

clinical_trials.title: ARIA: Phase 1/2 Study of REM-422 in Patients With Recurrent or Metastatic Adenoid Cystic Carcinoma
sites: Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center, New York
biomarker_requirements: MYB transcript expression with poison exon
clinical_trials.early_results: 43% ORR in efficacy population; 71% of biomarker-positive patients on study >6 months showed >20% tumor shrinkage; FDA Fast Track and Orphan Drug designations granted; first-in-class MYB mRNA degrader with proof-of-mechanism demonstrated

clinical_trials.title: Phase I/II Study of CB-103 Combined with Lenvatinib or Abemaciclib in Advanced Solid Tumors Including ACC
clinical_trials.early_results: Ongoing enrollment; CB-103 is a NOTCH transcription complex inhibitor with potential to avoid GI toxicity seen with gamma-secretase inhibitors; combination with lenvatinib targets dual oncogenic pathways

clinical_trials.title: Phase II Study of 177Lu-PSMA Radioligand Therapy in Adenoid Cystic Carcinoma
clinical_trials.early_results: PSMA expression confirmed in 94% of primary ACC cases; leverages theranostic approach validated in prostate cancer; no efficacy data published yet

clinical_trials.title: Phase I Study of OBT076 (Anti-CD205 ADC) +/- Balstilimab in Solid Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: First-in-human study; antibody-drug conjugate targeting CD205; no published efficacy data yet; optional combination with anti-PD-1 checkpoint inhibitor

clinical_trials.title: Phase II Study of Nivolumab and Ipilimumab for Metastatic/Recurrent ACC and Salivary Gland Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: 19 ACC patients: median OS 30.0 months, median PFS 8.3 months, DCR 53%; PFS superior to historical single-agent checkpoint data (4.9-6.6 months with pembrolizumab); represents most promising immunotherapy data in ACC

clinical_trials.title: Phase I Study of AL-107 (NOTCH Inhibitor) in Advanced Solid Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: Next-generation NOTCH pathway targeting; 14 patients planned; follow-on to ACCURACY trial experience with AL101

clinical_trials.title: Phase II Study of 9-ING-41 (GSK-3beta Inhibitor) + Carboplatin in ACC
clinical_trials.early_results: GSK-3beta inhibitor combined with platinum chemotherapy; 35 patients planned; novel mechanism targeting glycogen synthase kinase pathway

combination_strategies.rationale: Concurrent cisplatin with proton therapy for unresectable or high-risk (T3, extensive perineural invasion) ACC has shown enhanced locoregional control; proton dose conformality allows skull base coverage while platinum radiosensitization improves local control; 9-patient series showed 4 complete responses and 4 disease stabilizations

Preclinical studies demonstrate vorinostat unlocks chromatin of ACC cancer stem cells, sensitizing them to cisplatin; combination depletes cancer stem cells and induces tumor senescence; addresses the CSC-driven chemoresistance that limits cytotoxic chemotherapy efficacy in ACC; synergistic effect observed in all ACC primary cell lines tested

Anti-angiogenic agents may normalize tumor vasculature and enhance immune cell infiltration, potentially overcoming ACC's immunosuppressive microenvironment; 64-patient Phase II trial tested this combination; however results did not show ORR superiority over lenvatinib alone, suggesting ACC's low immunogenicity limits checkpoint inhibitor contribution

CDK6 expression is 4-fold higher in ACC than squamous cell carcinoma; NOTCH pathway activation drives cell cycle entry through CDK4/6; dual inhibition of NOTCH transcription and CDK4/6 may produce synthetic lethal interaction; particularly relevant for NOTCH1-activated ACC

Targeting the MYB-NFIB driver fusion with REM-422 while simultaneously blocking VEGFR-mediated angiogenesis (lenvatinib) or NOTCH-driven proliferation could achieve multi-pathway suppression; addresses the key molecular drivers of this patient's tumor (MYB-NFIB fusion + NOTCH1 activation)

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Head and Neck Cancer guidelines recommend pre-treatment and post-treatment swallowing evaluation for all tongue base cancer patients; prospective studies show early intervention preserves swallowing function and reduces aspiration risk
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents and manages dysphagia secondary to surgery and radiation of the tongue base; maintains oral nutrition; reduces aspiration pneumonia risk; preserves speech intelligibility; critical for quality of life in tongue base cancer patients

supportive_care.evidence: ACC's hallmark perineural invasion causes significant neuropathic pain; NCCN supportive care guidelines recommend gabapentinoids as first-line for cancer-related neuropathic pain; multidisciplinary pain management improves QoL outcomes
supportive_care.benefit: Manages pain from perineural invasion along cranial nerves; reduces need for opioid analgesia; addresses post-surgical and post-radiation neuropathy; improves functional status and sleep quality

supportive_care.approach: Xerostomia Management (Salivary Substitutes, Pilocarpine, Dental Prophylaxis)
supportive_care.evidence: Radiation to the tongue base and parotid regions causes xerostomia in >80% of patients; systematic reviews support pilocarpine and cevimeline for stimulating residual salivary function; proton therapy reduces but does not eliminate xerostomia risk
supportive_care.benefit: Mitigates radiation-induced xerostomia; preserves dental health and prevents post-radiation caries; maintains oral comfort and nutritional intake; pilocarpine increases salivary flow in patients with residual gland function

supportive_care.evidence: ASCO and NCCN guidelines endorse exercise during and after cancer treatment; meta-analyses demonstrate exercise reduces cancer-related fatigue by 25-30% and may improve overall survival across cancer types; head and neck cancer-specific data shows improved functional outcomes
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces cancer-related fatigue; maintains muscle mass during treatment; improves cardiovascular fitness; may enhance treatment tolerance; associated with improved mood and reduced anxiety; potential survival benefit

supportive_care.approach: Long-Term Surveillance for Late Distant Metastases (Pulmonary Monitoring)
supportive_care.evidence: ACC characteristically develops late distant metastases (especially lung) 5-15+ years after initial treatment; NCCN recommends lifelong surveillance with periodic chest imaging; 10-year distant metastasis rate approximately 38%
supportive_care.benefit: Early detection of pulmonary metastases enables consideration of metastasectomy, SBRT, or systemic therapy at lower disease burden; ACC lung metastases can be indolent and amenable to local therapy when detected early; 10-15 year follow-up is essential

supportive_care.evidence: Head and neck cancer patients undergoing surgery and radiation are at high risk for malnutrition; ESPEN guidelines recommend nutritional assessment and intervention for all head and neck cancer patients; prophylactic PEG tube placement may be considered
supportive_care.benefit: Maintains body weight and nutritional status during treatment; reduces treatment interruptions; improves wound healing post-surgery; PEG tube provides reliable nutrition access during severe mucositis phases

Perineural Invasion in Head and Neck ACC: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
ACC of Base of Tongue: Population-Based Study
